div.p: Leader → Follower message format for log replication
Leader → Follower Sent by leader to replicate log entries and maintain authority (heartbeat)
term int Leader's current term number Used to detect stale leaders
leaderId string Unique identifier of the leader Enables client redirect
prevLogIndex int Index of log entry immediately preceding new entries
prevLogTerm int Term of the entry at prevLogIndex CRITICAL: inductive consistency check
entries []LogEntry Log entries to store Empty = heartbeat
leaderCommit int Leader's current commitIndex Follower updates its commitIndex
term int Follower's current term Leader updates if follower's term > own
success bool true = consistency check passed false = log mismatch, retry needed
conflictTerm int (optimization) Term of conflicting entry Speeds up log backtracking
conflictIndex int (optimization) First index where conflictTerm appears Enables faster nextIndex adjustment
term Detects stale leaders. If args.term < currentTerm, follower rejects. If args.term > currentTerm, follower updates term and steps down.
prevLogIndex + prevLogTerm The inductive consistency check. Proves logs are identical up to prevLogIndex. If follower's log at prevLogIndex has different term → reject.
entries[] New log entries to append. Empty array = heartbeat. Follower truncates conflicting entries, then appends.
leaderCommit Follower sets commitIndex = min(leaderCommit, index of last new entry). Ensures followers advance commit index when leader commits.
conflictTerm + conflictIndex Optimization: Without this, leader decrements nextIndex by 1 per failure. With this, leader jumps directly to conflict point (like TCP SACK).
